content:
**Brief Answer**  
Yes—this plan includes Pregnancy and Maternity Care benefits (prenatal care, postnatal care, complications of pregnancy, and inpatient hospital services for labor/delivery/postpartum). Your cost share depends on where services are provided and whether they are professional vs. facility charges.

---
**Detailed Explanation**

### Pregnancy and Maternity Care (general)
Pregnancy and maternity care benefits include:

Prenatal care;  
Postnatal care;  
Involuntary complications of pregnancy;  
Inpatient Hospital services including labor, delivery, and postpartum care

There can be additional fees for ultrasounds, services provided by a mid-wife and for the California Prenatal Screening Program (which is offered to check for genetic conditions and birth defects). Also, once the baby is born there could be newborn nursery charges. [1]

A. Pre & Postnatal Visits: [1]

B. Diagnostic imaging - Ultrasounds:  
1. Outpatient Radiology Center  
2. Office Location [1]

C. Delivery:  
1. Hospital  
2. Phyisican -  
3. Mid-wife non-RN  
4. Mid-wife RN  
5. Anesthesia [1]

D. Preventive Health - Prenatal Screen Program [1]

E. Newborn Services -   Nursery charges [1]

---

## A) Pre/Postnatal visits (Professional)
### In-Network Provider
However, Prenatal and postnatal visits are not subject to the deductible. [2]  
The plan will reimburse the provider 100% of the allowed amount for prenatal and postnatal visits. [2]

### Out-of-Network Provider
For out-of-network providers:

An out-of-network provider has the right to bill for the balance. What this means is that the member may be responsible for the difference between the allowed amount and billed amount. [3]  
the plan has a $1,500 deductible per person, not to exceed $3,000 per family. [4]  
Prenatal and postnatal visits is subject to this deductible. Once the deductible is met, [3]  
The coinsurance is 40% of the allowed amount, and the plan will reimburse the remaining 60% of the allowed amount for prenatal and postnatal visits. [3]  
The service applies to the plan's out-of-pocket maximum amount of $15,800, not to exceed $31,600 per family. When the plan's out-of-pocket maximum amount is satisfied, the health plan will reimburse 100% of the allowed amount. [3]

---

## B) Delivery – Inpatient Hospital (Facility)
### In-Network Provider
the plan has a $750 deductible per person, not to exceed $1,500 per family. [5]  
Hospital facility services for delivery is subject to this deductible. Once the deductible is met, [6]  
The coinsurance is 20% of the allowed amount, and the plan will reimburse the remaining 80% of the allowed amount for hospital facility services for delivery. [6]  
The service applies to the plan's out-of-pocket maximum amount of $7,900, not to exceed $15,800 per family. When the plan's out-of-pocket maximum amount is satisfied, the health plan will reimburse 100% of the allowed amount. [6]

In addition to the hospital bill, the member can expect related professional fees for services like surgery, anesthesia, and diagnostic testing (radiological, pathology, or laboratory). [7]

### Out-of-Network Provider
For out-of-network providers:

An out-of-network provider has the right to bill for the balance. What this means is that the member may be responsible for the difference between the allowed amount and billed amount. [8]  
the plan has a $1,500 deductible per person, not to exceed $3,000 per family. [4]  
Hospital facility services for delivery is subject to this deductible. Once the deductible is met, [8]  
The coinsurance is 40% of the allowed amount, and the plan will reimburse the remaining 60% of the allowed amount for hospital facility services for delivery. [8]  
The service applies to the plan's out-of-pocket maximum amount of $15,800, not to exceed $31,600 per family. When the plan's out-of-pocket maximum amount is satisfied, the health plan will reimburse 100% of the allowed amount. [8]  
This plan has a maximum payable amount of up to $2000 per day. This means the member would also be responsible for all charges above $2000 per day. [8]

In addition to the hospital bill, the member can expect related professional fees for services like surgery, anesthesia, and diagnostic testing (radiological, pathology, or laboratory). [7]

---

## C) Delivery – Inpatient Professional Services (Physician/midwife/anesthesia)
### In-Network Provider
the plan has a $750 deductible per person, not to exceed $1,500 per family. [5]  
Inpatient delivery professional services is subject to this deductible. Once the deductible is met, [9]  
The coinsurance is 20% of the allowed amount, and the plan will reimburse the remaining 80% of the allowed amount for inpatient delivery professional services. [9]  
The service applies to the plan's out-of-pocket maximum amount of $7,900, not to exceed $15,800 per family. When the plan's out-of-pocket maximum amount is satisfied, the health plan will reimburse 100% of the allowed amount. [9]

### Out-of-Network Provider
For out-of-network providers:

An out-of-network provider has the right to bill for the balance. What this means is that the member may be responsible for the difference between the allowed amount and billed amount. [10]  
the plan has a $1,500 deductible per person, not to exceed $3,000 per family. [4]  
Inpatient delivery professional services is subject to this deductible. Once the deductible is met, [10]  
The coinsurance is 40% of the allowed amount, and the plan will reimburse the remaining 60% of the allowed amount for inpatient delivery professional services. [10]  
The service applies to the plan's out-of-pocket maximum amount of $15,800, not to exceed $31,600 per family. When the plan's out-of-pocket maximum amount is satisfied, the health plan will reimburse 100% of the allowed amount. [10]

---

## D) Newborn – Nursery charges (Inpatient Professional)
### In-Network Provider
the plan has a $750 deductible per person, not to exceed $1,500 per family. [5]  
Nursery charges is subject to this deductible. Once the deductible is met, [11]  
The coinsurance is 20% of the allowed amount, and the plan will reimburse the remaining 80% of the allowed amount for nursery charges. [11]  
The service applies to the plan's out-of-pocket maximum amount of $7,900, not to exceed $15,800 per family. When the plan's out-of-pocket maximum amount is satisfied, the health plan will reimburse 100% of the allowed amount. [11]

### Out-of-Network Provider
For out-of-network providers:

An out-of-network provider has the right to bill for the balance. What this means is that the member may be responsible for the difference between the allowed amount and billed amount. [12]  
the plan has a $1,500 deductible per person, not to exceed $3,000 per family. [4]  
Nursery charges is subject to this deductible. Once the deductible is met, [12]  
The coinsurance is 40% of the allowed amount, and the plan will reimburse the remaining 60% of the allowed amount for nursery charges. [12]  
The service applies to the plan's out-of-pocket maximum amount of $15,800, not to exceed $31,600 per family. When the plan's out-of-pocket maximum amount is satisfied, the health plan will reimburse 100% of the allowed amount. [12]
